column1: West Asheville Tailgate Market and River Arts District Market
Brian Santos personal notes, updated January 2026
Location: Haywood Road near Clingman Avenue
Season: Year-round, Saturday 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM (winter hours 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM)

column5: Best eggs at the market. Sell out by 9:30 AM. Arrive early.
Lacinato kale, tomatoes, winter squash. Know Brian by name now.
Seeded sourdough, levain. Good when I am not baking.
Raw wildflower honey. Buy the 1 lb jar, lasts 6 weeks.
Chamomile, basil, kale starts in spring. Best seed source locally.
Brian buys the ginger or elderflower. Owen likes the berry.
Good for Neil when he wants something sweet.
Brian buys pork chops occasionally. Not weekly.
Spring and summer tulips, zinnias. Occasional treat.
Shiitakes, oysters. For soups, good quality.

column1: Season: Saturday 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM year-round
Notes: Larger market, more vendors, less familiar faces. Good for variety.

column3: Summer only. The best tomatoes in the city.
Brian occasionally buys the lavender salve for dry hands.
Morels in spring if you get lucky. More expensive than market.
Good winter mix when Whippoorwill is light on greens.
Not food, but Brian bought the tea mug she uses every day here.

column1: Arrive by 8:00 AM at West Asheville market, 8:10 at the latest.
First stop always: Peaceful Valley Farm for eggs.
Second: Whippoorwill for vegetables and whatever looks good.
Third: Roots Bakery if not baking.
Honey when the current jar is running low.
Budget: $60 to $80 at the market, $20 to $40 at Ingles for staples.
The kids come most Saturdays in summer. Owen eats half the cherry tomatoes before we get home.
